perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Agent pursuant to this Agreement shall terminate upon termination of the Offering, but in no event later than 45 days after the completion of the Subscription Offering (the "End Date"). All fees or expenses due to the Agent but unpaid will be payable to the Agent in next day funds at the earlier of the Closing Date (as hereinafter defined) or the End Date.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Agent pursuant to this Agreement shall terminate upon termination of the Offering, but in no event later than 90 days after the completion of the Subscription Offering (the "End Date"). All fees or expenses due to the Agent but unpaid will be payable to the Agent in next day funds 30 days after the Closing Date (as hereinafter defined) or the End Date.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 2 creates an ambiguity regarding the end date and when the payment is due. Originally, the Agent's obligations ended 45 days post-subscription offering, and payment was due at the earlier of the Closing Date or End Date. Now, it's 90 days post-subscription offering, and payment is due 30 days after the Closing Date or the End Date. This introduces an in-text contradiction with the contract potentially having different terms in other sections being in effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event the Offering is extended beyond the End Date, the Company and the Agent may agree to renew this Agreement under mutually acceptable terms. In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of Shares within the period herein provided, this Agreement shall terminate and the Company shall refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares the full amount which it may have received from them plus accrued interest, as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 7, 9 and 10 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The modified text alters the End Date from 45 to 90 days post-subscription offering and changes the payment due date to 30 days after the Closing Date, creating a contradiction with the original terms. This directly conflicts with the contradicted section, which refers to the 'End Date' for termination and refund obligations. The change introduces ambiguity and inconsistency in critical dates, potentially causing confusion and disputes regarding the agent's obligations, the company's refund responsibilities, and the duration of the agreement. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Certificates for shares shall be delivered 3 directly to the purchasers in accordance with their directions. The date upon which the Company shall release or deliver the shares sold in the Offering, in accordance with the terms herein, is called the "Closing Date."
perturbation.changed_text: Certificates for shares shall be delivered 3 directly to the purchasers only upon explicit approval by the Company's Board of Directors, regardless of purchaser directions. The date upon which the Company shall release or deliver the shares sold in the Offering, in accordance with the terms herein, is called the "Closing Date."
perturbation.explanation: The modification restricts the delivery of share certificates, requiring board approval which can override the purchaser's directions. The agent is appointed to utilize its best efforts to solicit subscriptions for Common Shares and advise and assist the Company and Bank with respect to the Company’s sale of Shares. This makes it unclear how the company intends to execute this role when the company is restricting and or limiting the powers of the agent on how those certificates are being sent. If the agent is a non-exclusive agent, then it becomes unclear on how that works as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Company and the Bank hereby appoint the Agent as their exclusive financial advisor and marketing agent (i) to utilize its best efforts to solicit subscriptions for Common Shares and to advise and assist the Company and the Bank with respect to the Company's sale of the Shares in the Offering and (ii) to participate in the Offering in the areas of market making and in syndicate formation (if necessary).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that certificates for shares shall be delivered directly to the purchasers in accordance with their directions. The changed text introduces a requirement for explicit approval by the Company's Board of Directors, regardless of purchaser directions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which appoints the Agent to utilize its best efforts to solicit subscriptions and assist with the Company's sale of Shares. The agent's ability to fulfill this role is undermined by the company's control over share certificate delivery, creating a clear conflict and ambiguity about the agent's authority and responsibilities. Thus this is an Ambiguities-In Text Contradiction.
